lesson: On MSYS/Git Bash, using sed for backslash-to-forward-slash conversion (e.g. sed 's|backslash|/|g') crashes with 'unterminated s command'. This has broken the harness TWICE.
fix: Use tr for backslash-to-forward-slash conversion instead of sed.

lesson: In THIS project, harness/beast state (the disabled flag) MUST be resolved by PROJECT ROOT, never relative to the current working directory. Reading a bare .claude/state/harness-disabled.flag from cwd caused nested-root lockouts in claude-mesh/seo/api (fixed in commit 99353ff).
fix: Resolve upward to the nearest directory containing .claude using the find_project_state_dir / harness_disabled_resolved helpers in lib-helpers.sh; never hard-code a cwd-relative .claude/state path.

lesson: Install pack: ship FIXED headroom always-on by default (health-gated)
fix: Install pack: ship FIXED headroom always-on by default (health-gated) (see commit 95f873c)
dossier:
commit 95f873c � files: _install/README.md _install/install.sh _install/scripts/headroom-alwayson.sh _install/scripts/headroom-supervisor.ps1 _install/scripts/headroom-supervisor.sh tests/test-headroom-last30days-integration.sh 
headroom is fixed (HEADROOM_RUST_DETECT=0 stopped the compression hang; agent-90
profile; model downloads/prefetches fine). Re-enable it in the pack:

- install.sh step 9: install ON by default (HEADROOM_INSTALL=0 to skip) and
  always-on ON by default (HEADROOM_ALWAYSON=0 to stay opt-in via claude-hr.sh).
- New supervisor watchdogs headroom-supervisor.{ps1,sh}: keep the proxy alive
  across reboots (Windows logon task / Linux systemd --user or @reboot cron),
  baking RUST_DETECT=0 + agent-90.
- New headroom-alwayson.sh enable/disable: prefetch model, install autostart,
  then HEALTH-GATE � write ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL ONLY after the proxy answers
  /livez. If anything is unhealthy the redirect is NOT set, so no server is ever
  bricked (the 2026-06-13 incident lesson). 'disable' cleanly un-bricks.
- Shipped settings.json still carries NO redirect (enabler sets it post-health).

Tests: 37/0 (H7 series now assert default-on + health-gated + supervisor fix +
shipped-settings-clean). Synced live.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Fix Linux compatibility across 7 _install scripts (see commit 8ac072d)
dossier:
commit 8ac072d � files: _install/hooks/collect-test-evidence.sh _install/scripts/check-coverage.sh _install/scripts/detect-test-runner.sh _install/scripts/test-hook-output.sh _install/scripts/validate-phase.sh _install/scripts/validate-pre-flight.sh _install/scripts/wiki-dropbox.sh

  pkill on Linux)
- Replace hardcoded G:/harness infra path in test-hook-output.sh with

- Replace hardcoded G:/AgentWiki in wiki-dropbox.sh with

- Replace hardcoded /g mount in check-coverage.sh with
  ${HARNESS_SEARCH_PATH:-/g}

lesson: Fix validate-phase.sh printf bug: dash-prefixed grep output treated as option
fix: Fix validate-phase.sh printf bug: dash-prefixed grep output treated as option (see commit 18c3fb3)
dossier:
commit 18c3fb3 � files: _install/scripts/validate-phase.sh 
printf "- %s\n" fails when $ERR_LINE starts with a dash � printf interprets
it as an option flag. Fix: printf -- "- %s\n" (add -- to end option parsing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

lesson: Fix validate-phase.sh: sprint stored as string not matched by numeric grep
fix: Fix validate-phase.sh: sprint stored as string not matched by numeric grep (see commit 4167683)
dossier:
commit 4167683 � files: _install/scripts/validate-phase.sh 
Ledger entries store sprint as "sprint":"22" (string) but validate-phase.sh
grep'd for "sprint":22 (number). Result: zero matches, permanent FAIL on
BUILD phase completion. Fix: grep -E "sprint":"?N"?" matches both formats.

Tested: old pattern = 0 matches, new pattern = 2 matches on real ledger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

lesson: Fix pre-flight gate deadlock: add .claude/evidence/ to phase-feedback exemptions
fix: Fix pre-flight gate deadlock: add .claude/evidence/ to phase-feedback exemptions (see commit 2966f05)
dossier:
commit 2966f05 � files: .claude/contracts/sprint-25-contract.md _install/hooks/pre-flight-gate.sh 
Evidence files are harness infrastructure, not source code. Without this exemption,
agents hit a circular deadlock: phase-feedback FAIL blocks evidence writes, but
clearing the feedback requires evidence-verdict.json to exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

lesson: Sprint 40: fix beast-mode relevance (precise triggers + working semantic recall)
fix: Sprint 40: fix beast-mode relevance (precise triggers + working semantic recall) (see commit 0600641)
dossier:
commit 0600641 � files: .beast/lessons.jsonl .claude/evidence/sprint-40-independent-validation.md .claude/evidence/sprint-40-relevance-evidence.md .claude/reports/sprint-40-relevance-tuning-report.md _install/scripts/beast-mp-recall.sh _install/scripts/beast-pack.sh tests/test-beast-mp-cache.sh tests/test-beast-mp-integration.sh tests/test-beast-pack-quality.sh tests/test-beast-pack.sh tests/test-beast-recall-wiring.sh tests/test-beast-watch-hook.sh 
Audit of real PCW use showed beast surfaced ~62 injections almost all from one wildcard-scope lesson with a common-word trigger (intro|system|working...), while the semantic layer fired 0 times (mempalace took 5-8s vs a 4s hook timeout). Fixes: (1) beast-pack mk_trigger keeps DISTINCTIVE tokens only (expanded STOP list), skips harness/state-file commits, skips no-anchor lessons, no wildcard scopes -> harness-infra re-packed with 0 noisy triggers. (2) beast-mp-recall queries mempalace UNSCOPED + filters wing client-side (the --wing filter breaks/slows after a mine), subprocess-free parser (16s -> 2.5s), cosine treated as similarity (>=0.40), query cache (cold 2.5s -> warm 0.7s), and filters out beast's own injected packets that got mined back as memory. (3) mined harness-infra into a g__harness_infra wing so the semantic layer has project memory. Empirically tuned on harness-infra's OWN files: lib-helpers->killswitch, pre-write-gate->must-do, etc. all relevant; unrelated query silent. TDD: pack-quality 7/7, mp-cache 8/8; full beast regression 157/0. Independent validator PASS (5/5 files relevant, control silent, read-only, parity). Literal-layer tests isolated from the semantic layer + cache.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

lesson: Sprint 49: fix must-do ownership churn (greedy resolver) + full heartbeat coverage
fix: Sprint 49: fix must-do ownership churn (greedy resolver) + full heartbeat coverage (see commit 9624563)
dossier:
commit 9624563 � files: .claude/reports/sprint-49-mustdo-ownership-churn-and-heartbeat.md _install/hooks/pre-bash-gate.sh _install/hooks/pre-flight-gate.sh _install/hooks/pre-write-gate.sh _install/scripts/lib-helpers.sh tests/test-mustdo-fanout-gate.sh tests/test-mustdo-ownership-churn.sh 
Live PCW session stuck looping pre-flight ('slot reset') + must-do ('no grounding'). Root: (1) the ordinal-by-slot resolver handed a lower-slot NEW session (2f081509 slot1) the must-do.md a higher-slot session (d2248c13 slot2) had already grounded -> collision -> orphaned grounding -> loop; (2) heartbeat only fired on on-prompt-submit + successful Write/Edit, so a Bash-heavy or blocked-write turn went stale and got its watcher+summary reaped mid-work. Fix: greedy ownership-aware resolver -- a file stamped by me is mine (stable), else claim the lowest file NOT owned by a LIVE peer (unstamped/mine/dead-owner), ties by slot rank; session_is_live now treats an active watcher with no timestamp as LIVE (fail-safe, matches reap). Heartbeat (watcher_touch_session) now fires early in pre-write-gate + pre-bash-gate + pre-flight-gate (before blocking), so any tool attempt keeps the watcher alive. TDD: test-mustdo-ownership-churn 7/0 (reported case: lower-slot new session -> must-do-2.md not must-do.md; stable; two-new distinct; dead-owner reclaim; bash heartbeat). Full regression 18 suites/0 (fanout-gate CONTROL updated: B never routed to a live peer's file even without a watcher). Independent validator PASS A-G, all 4 fail-critical invariants; slot-order not array-order; byte parity 4/4.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

lesson: Sprint 45: default per-session must-do file fan-out (fix multi-session OWNERSHIP deadlock)
fix: Sprint 45: default per-session must-do file fan-out (fix multi-session OWNERSHIP deadlock) (see commit 88834aa)
dossier:
commit 88834aa � files: .claude/reports/sprint-45-mustdo-default-fanout-fix-report.md _install/hooks/on-prompt-submit.sh _install/hooks/post-write-check.sh _install/hooks/pre-bash-gate.sh _install/hooks/pre-write-gate.sh _install/scripts/lib-helpers.sh tests/test-mustdo-default-fanout.sh tests/test-mustdo-fanout-gate.sh 
Two sessions in one project deadlocked: both owned the single must-do.md, each saw the other's stamp as foreign, each +++pack archived the other's file and re-stamped -> ping-pong. Root cause: mustdo_file_for_dir fanned out ONLY by LANE, and multilane is opt-in (.claude/multi-lane.json) that agents never enable, so every session was LANE=1 -> shared must-do.md. Fix (default, no opt-in): mustdo_file_for_dir now returns each session's OWN file -- a file already stamped by me (stable), else assigned by my ordinal among the project's ACTIVE watcher sessions sorted by slot (must-do.md, must-do-2.md, ...), race-free since slots are uniquely assigned. Threaded via HARNESS_SESSION_ID set per hook from payload .session_id. The system now TELLS each agent its own file: on-prompt-submit BUILD packet always names it ('YOUR must-do file is X -- yours alone, never overwrite another session's'), and the OWNERSHIP block names 'YOUR OWN must-do file is: X'. Also fixed pre-write-gate:379 which clobbered a resolved-but-not-yet-existing owned file (must-do-2.md) with find|head -1 (=must-do.md), re-creating the deadlock at the gate; and silenced a phantom 'No such file' from a reader loop. TDD: test-mustdo-default-fanout 9/0, test-mustdo-fanout-gate 4/0 (end-to-end CONTROL reproduces deadlock, FIX breaks it via pre-write AND pre-bash). Regression: session-aware 23/0, session-owned 10/0, default-on 11/0, pack 25/0, killswitch 22/0. Independent validator PASS (A-E; 20-way concurrent resolves, zero collisions; slot-driven). Live<->_install byte parity 5/5.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
